taylor-williams.female_specific_note: Taylor Williams Coaching focuses on female-specific HRT/peptide guidance. Per-peptide notes here often emphasize female dosing, fertility, hormone interactions.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider PE-22-28 primarily as a mood / antidepressant-class neuropeptide delivered intranasally, with both Hunter Williams and Taylor Williams describing it as a 'little bit more of an antidepressant' that can be paired with PE-21 but is preferably cycled separately. We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh that the experts in our source set never published a specific PE-22-28 dose in milligrams or micrograms — the only operative parameters they confirm are route (nasal spray) and indication (mood support, serotonin/neurotransmitter modulation, brain-inflammation reduction). We'd defer all numeric dosing to the prescribing clinician's research-grade source rather than infer one.
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- No verbatim dose was given by Hunter, Taylor, or Trevor in the source set — `top_doses` extracted by the mention parser (100 mg, 1 IU) are artifacts of unrelated neighboring Q&A about test and GH cycling at the same timestamp; do NOT use them as PE-22-28 dosing.
- Dr Trevor Bachmeyer has zero mentions of PE-22-28 in the indexed SmashweRx content — no chronic / acute counterweight from that voice.
- Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:43:41.440): "Yes, you can use both of those as nasal spray. Um you could probably do them together and it's fine. I would just use them separately cuz they do a little bit different things." — pairing PE-22-28 with PE-21 is permitted but not preferred.
- Taylor (hgI5Mpc-xDQ 01:40:24.560): "I would actually be very interested to see how PE 2228 does in people because it does work kind of like an anti-depressant peptide" — explicit hedge: she has not yet observed it in her own client base, the framing is theoretical.
- Hunter does NOT have a 2023 cheat-sheet baseline_protocol for PE-22-28 — there is no older floor to override; the entire signal is 2026 content.
- Indication overlaps with traditional antidepressant pharmacotherapy. We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh interaction risk with SSRIs / SNRIs / MAO inhibitors before stacking.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus:
Hunter and Taylor agree on three points: (1) PE-22-28 is intranasal; (2) it acts in the antidepressant / mood class; (3) it is distinct from PE-21 and worth using on its own cycle.

- Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:43:51.040): "PE-22-28 is a little bit more of an antidepressant. PE-21 is a little bit more of just a modulator of neurotransmitters helping with energy in the brain, helping with reducing inflammation in the brain."
- Taylor (Cg7JtpnfRY4 01:43:51.040): "PE-22-28 is a little bit more of an antidepressant. P21 is a little bit more of just a modulator of neurotransmitters helping with energy in the brain, helping with reducing inflammation in the brain." (Hunter and Taylor co-host the 4/18/26 Coffee Talk; both YouTube uploads carry the same dialogue.)
- Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 02:01:54.040): "Yep, PE-22-28 nasal. Big help with mood? That's what it does."
- Taylor (Cg7JtpnfRY4 02:01:54.040): "Yep, PE-22-28 nasal. Big help with mood? That's what it does."
- Taylor (hgI5Mpc-xDQ 01:40:30.080): "it does work kind of like an anti-depressant peptide um to help people" — Taylor's earlier 2/28/26 framing is consistent with the joint 4/18/26 framing.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: No meaningful disagreement was found in the source set. Hunter and Taylor co-host the 4/18/26 episode and deliver the same answer to the same listener question (Jim's nasal-spray question at 01:43). Taylor's standalone 2/28/26 framing (PE-22-28 as 'serotonin' / 'anti-depressant peptide') is consistent rather than contradictory. Trevor is absent from the source set so no acute-vs-chronic counterweight is available — call this a single-camp consensus, not validated triangulation.

chronic.dose: Not specified by the experts in the source set — practitioner-discretion based on a research-grade source.
